article_meta.title: Police turned him over to ICE

reasoning: Mixed quality: it correctly flags several biased elements in the author’s prose, but commits an attribution violation by flagging a quoted statement, mislabels at least one instance, and misses multiple clear author-level bias phrases (e.g., headline/subhead sensationalism and subjective qualifiers).

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: One clear violation: it flags a direct quote (“It does not feel like justice for anybody.”) as author bias.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: One definite false positive (the quoted line). Another item (Bias by Omission) is debatable given some agency responses were included.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Misses several prominent author-prose biases: sensational headline/subhead (“hail of bullets”), subjective qualifier (“controversial agreement”), loaded phrasing (“crosshairs of a determined gunman,” “uncorked a wave of controversy,” “bristled”).
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Several labels are appropriate (Slant; Sensationalism). At least one mislabel (“ripped apart” better fits Sensationalism/Word Choice than Subjective Qualifying Adjectives), and the Omission label is questionable.
